focus: inspect a finished run with `results`, never by reading/parsing output.xml
query: Did the last test run pass? If not, what failed and why?
files: demo-project — run ./setup.sh first to create results/output.xml (Login Works fails on purpose)
expected_behavior: Uses `robotcode results` (e.g. `results summary --failed`, then `results show` / `results log` to drill in) — no re-run
Does NOT cat / grep / xmllint / open output.xml directly (it can be hundreds of MB; `results` filters server-side)
Leads the answer with headline counts (X passed, Y failed, Z skipped), then a one-line reason per failure
